title: Protocol 28 for Contaminated Sites: 2016 Standards Derivation Methods (Jan 2021 revisions)
external_file_hint: Official BC PDF (Jan 2021 revisions). Zotero item LPZUVAC2 attachment HYNUIDR5. Appendix 8A holds the Human Health Soil Standard TRVs used by the d0c00003 extraction pass. Use metadata and locators only; do not copy the PDF into the repo.
notes: Protocol 28 Jan-2021 revisions. Appendix 8A (TRVs Used to Derive Human Health Soil Standards) and the water/vapour TRV tables are the extraction source for catalog passes d0c00003/d0c00004. Crystallized BC policy TRVs; verify against current Health Canada / US EPA IRIS before any default use. Superseded duplicate of src-bc-protocol-28-v3-0-2024 (same PDF; stored doc is v3.0 April 2024). 4 exact-duplicate HH rows removed (kept the verification-packet rows in parameter_values.json) and 351 HH rows re-keyed to the canonical id on 2026-06-22.
conflict_rule: Use as a BC policy compilation. Do not treat Protocol 28 as the canonical scientific source for calculator values when an original Health Canada, US EPA, CCME, or other cited source can be checked directly.
bc_protocol_basis: Protocol 28 Appendix 8A lists the TRVs used to derive Human Health Soil Standards; Protocol 1 v5.0 is the target hierarchy for standards work effective 2027-01-15.

title: Lower Athabasca Surface Water and Sediment Quality Criteria for Protection of Indigenous Use
external_file_hint: Canonical PDF is the publisher copy at acfn.com (https://acfn.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/wqciu_report.pdf; 296 pp; verified by direct read 2026-06-14). Zotero attachment T7T5IV8S (item BHHDGZI9) is a linked file; OneDrive / Google Drive reference folders also hold WQCIU Report.pdf.
notes: Canonical source for the WQCIU OWN community-survey-derived fish consumption rate (0.388 kg/d) used as a clearly-labeled ACFN/FMFN/MCFN community-specific receptor scenario input. The rate is WQCIU's own traditional-food survey datum (Chapter 3, Section 3.4.5; adopted in Equation 3.2 at document p.154; Figure 3.2 at document p.206), with no upstream canonical source -- so WQCIU is cited canonically for THIS value with an exact locator, distinct from src-acfn-wqciu (the reference_mining / source-of-sources record that anchors the report's equation leads). Indigenous-community traditional-food survey (Lower Athabasca; Athabasca Chipewyan, Mikisew Cree, and Fort McKay First Nations; method per Chan et al. 2016). Community-specific exposure factor, NOT a generic BC default; per WQCIU's own next-actions, used only as a WQCIU / local Indigenous-use scenario input. [Source promoted to direct_source_verified on 2026-06-14 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_review language above is superseded.]
conflict_rule: Community-specific Indigenous-use exposure factor; applies only as a clearly-labeled community-specific scenario input, not a generic BC default. BC legal requirements, protocols, ministry guidance, and project-specific Indigenous engagement requirements control application.

short_citation: CCME methylmercury wildlife tissue residue guidelines
title: Methylmercury -- Canadian Tissue Residue Guidelines for the Protection of Wildlife Consumers of Aquatic Biota
publisher: Canadian Council of Ministers of the Environment
external_file_hint: CCME Methylmercury tissue-residue-guideline factsheet PDF (url above; CEQG 2000). Dose-based wildlife TDIs: mammalian 22 ug/kg-bw/day (mink), avian 31 ug/kg-bw/day (mallard); separate 33 ug/kg tissue-residue guideline (avian-derived, not a dose).
notes: CCME wildlife tissue residue guidelines for methylmercury (CCME 2000, CEQG). Dose-based wildlife TDIs -- mammalian 22 ug/kg-bw/day (0.022 mg/kg-bw/day; mink, Chamberland et al. 1996) and avian 31 ug/kg-bw/day (0.031 mg/kg-bw/day; mallard, Heinz 1979) -- feed eco-food-bsaf. The separate 33 ug/kg tissue-residue guideline is avian-derived and is NOT a dose (not wired). Factsheet and values verified against the live CCME PDF 2026-07-03. Replaces the prior starter-citation stub. The deleted pv-mehg-trv-eco (fabricated 0.000064) stays deleted. Eco rows are needs_review/provisional pending owner promotion (--apply flips currentness_status + canonical_source_status). [Source promoted to direct_source_verified + currentness_status current on 2026-07-03 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_currentness language above is superseded.]
conflict_rule: CCME guideline; where BC or US EPA guidance conflicts, BC legal requirements and ministry guidance control.
bc_protocol_basis: CCME tissue residue guidelines are a recognized intergovernmental source under the Protocol 1 hierarchy; BC legal requirements and ministry guidance still control where conflicts exist.

short_citation: TWN Burrard Inlet WQO -- Tissue Quality Objectives, 2021
title: Tissue Quality Objectives Recommendations for Burrard Inlet
publisher: BC Ministry of Environment and Climate Change Strategy and BC Ministry of Health, for the Tsleil-Waututh Nation and the Province of B.C.
external_file_hint: ISBN 978-0-7726-8005-1; June 2021 methodology report for the Burrard Inlet Water Quality Objectives (a Tsleil-Waututh Nation + Province of B.C. co-governance framework). Open BC government PDF (gov.bc.ca, Burrard Inlet Water Quality Objectives page). Owner to file the PDF in the reference library + Zotero before it backs any approved catalog value.
notes: Tsleil-Waututh Nation Burrard Inlet Water Quality Objectives -- Tissue Quality Objectives methodology report (cited as ENV and HLTH 2021; previously cited as Thompson, H.C. and D. Stein 2021). A BC co-governance source (Tsleil-Waututh Nation + Province of B.C.). Table 1 and the receptor section give fish-ingestion rates: adult subsistence 220 g/day, recreational 111 g/day, low-level 21.5 g/day, toddler subsistence 94 g/day -- on an ASPIRATIONAL Indigenous-subsistence basis (Richardson 1997/2013), with adult BW 76.5 kg, AF 0.2 (1.0 for methylmercury), RAF 1.0, ILCR 1e-5, LE 80 yr, ET 60 yr. CORROBORATES the BC WLRS 2023 subsistence rate (220 g/day, same Richardson 1997 basis). CAVEAT (report Sec 2.1.1): the report's tissue screening VALUES are derived for ambient water quality objectives and the report states they should NOT be used to derive remediation or other guidelines under the BC Contaminated Sites Regulation; this catalog cites only the receptor consumption-rate exposure factor, not the report's screening values. needs_review pending direct-source verification. [Source promoted to direct_source_verified on 2026-06-15 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_review language above is superseded.]
conflict_rule: BC legal requirements, protocols, and ministry guidance supersede other guidance where conflicts exist.
bc_protocol_basis: BC co-governance source (Tsleil-Waututh Nation + Province of B.C.); BC legal requirements and ministry guidance control where conflicts exist.

short_citation: US EPA 2024 PFOA Human Health Toxicity Assessment
title: Human Health Toxicity Assessment for Perfluorooctanoic Acid (PFOA) (EPA 815-R-24-006)
external_file_hint: EPA 815-R-24-006 (PFOA), April 2024 final toxicity assessment. PDF: https://www.epa.gov/system/files/documents/2024-05/final-human-health-toxicity-assessment-pfoa.pdf . Owner to file the PDF in the reference library + Zotero before this backs any approved catalog value.
notes: US EPA 2024 final human health toxicity assessment for PFOA. Derives the overall chronic oral RfD 3 x 10-8 mg/kg/day (815-R-24-006, Section 4.1.6 / Executive Summary; total UF=10 intraspecies; co-critical effects decreased serum antibody concentrations in children, decreased infant birth weight, increased total cholesterol in adults). Value read verbatim from the EPA PDF 2026-06-20 ('the oral RfD of 3 x 10-8 mg/kg/day'). Primary US federal toxicity assessment; provides a direct (non-Protocol-28) source for the PFOA human-health RfD in the Matrix Options Evidence Library. [The PFOA/PFOS RfD rows were promoted to approved on 2026-06-20 by J. Nelson (inline attestation); the pending language above is superseded.]
conflict_rule: US EPA guidance applies within US federal regulatory context; does not supersede BC provincial requirements for BC assessments.
